mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in PAYEMS signals expanding household income and corporate revenue breadth, so rising payroll employment predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS 3-month change has a nonpositive walk-forward Rank IC with 20-day S&P 500 returns over two consecutive 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above 5.0%, restrictive policy raises equity discount rates and tightens financing conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations deliver positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward cases or if average triggered returns exceed unconditional average returns.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE combined with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% signals labor deterioration while policy remains tight, so recession-risk repricing drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises at least 0.3pp over 3 months and FEDFUNDS > 4.5%
failure_condition: Reject if the joint trigger does not underperform the unconditional 60-day S&P 500 return by at least 1 percentage point in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A positive 6-month trend in INDPRO signals improving production volumes and operating leverage, so industrial production acceleration predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 6-month INDPRO momentum has a negative coefficient in walk-forward regressions of 60-day S&P 500 returns after transaction-date alignment.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST falls more than 10% year over year, residential investment weakness signals slowing cyclical demand and credit sensitivity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered HOUST contractions have median 60-day S&P 500 returns greater than the unconditional median in more than half of rolling 5-year test windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month deceleration in CPIAUCSL while FEDFUNDS remains above 5.0% signals disinflation relief and future easing optionality, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth falls versus prior 3 months and FEDFUNDS > 5.0%
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term between CPIAUCSL deceleration and high FEDFUNDS is not positive for 20-day returns in walk-forward regressions across two consecutive 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in RSAFS indicates resilient consumer spending and near-term revenue momentum, so rising retail sales predict positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered RSAFS growth episodes do not produce a positive mean 5-day S&P 500 return or if hit rate is below 50% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising GDPC1 combined with UNRATE below 4.0% signals broad real-demand expansion without labor-market stress, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth is positive and UNRATE < 4.0%
failure_condition: Reject if the joint growth-and-low-unemployment trigger has a 60-day S&P 500 average return less than or equal to the unconditional average in walk-forward tests.
